executiveSummary: This complaint details serious professional misconduct by a chartered accountant who operated under six irreconcilable conflicts of interest, swore a false affidavit, acted as Commissioner of Oaths for his own affidavit, and concealed a R5.4M stock disappearance.

complainants.names: Jacqueline Faucitt and Daniel James Faucitt

conflictsOfInterest.conflict: Trustee of Faucitt Family Trust AND Commissioner of Oaths for own affidavit
CFO of George Group AND Trustee of FFT (opposing interests)
Affidavit witness AND interested party in Trust assets
Ketoni insider AND FFT Trustee (R18.685M transaction)
Distribution controller AND beneficiary of distribution scheme
Auditor role AND active participant in scheme

breaches.breach: Six irreconcilable conflicts of interest while acting in professional capacity
breaches.evidence: CIPC records, Trust deed, court filings

breaches.breach: Swore a false supporting affidavit to the High Court
Acted as Commissioner of Oaths for own affidavit (procedural impossibility)
breaches.evidence: Affidavit bearing his signature as both deponent and commissioner

breaches.breach: Concealed R5.4M stock disappearance from audit

reliefSought: Investigate the respondent's conduct and conflicts of interest.
Suspend the respondent's membership pending outcome of investigation.
Refer the matter for formal disciplinary hearing.
Report findings to the Independent Regulatory Board for Auditors (IRBA).
Notify all entities for which the respondent acts in a professional capacity.
